sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच। इत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः। संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम्॥
transliteration: sañjaya uvāca | ity ahaṃ vāsudevasya pārthasya ca mahātmanaḥ | saṃvādimam aśrauṣam adbhutaṃ romaharṣaṇam ||
literal_meaning: Sañjaya said: Thus I have heard the dialogue of Vāsudeva and the high-souled son of Pṛthā; this wondrous discourse makes my hair bristle.
contextual_meaning: The witness-mind (Sañjaya) reports that the encounter between the divine guide (Kṛṣṇa) and the struggling ego (Arjuna) produced an awe so intense it somatically overflows—pilo-erection as the body’s ‘truth-telling’ of transcendent meaning.

shadow: Fear of one’s own unlived potential

psychological_state: Post-dialogic integration: the observer ego registers cathartic completion and bodily confirmation that profound coherence has been restored.
trigger_scenario: After a breakthrough therapy session where client and therapist finally name the core wound, the reflecting team supervisor feels goose-bumps and says, ‘I’ve never heard anything like this; something just shifted.’
anchor_text: When truth speaks, the body listens and hair stands in silent salute.
